primary_diagnosis.name: Acute colicky flare of microscopic (lymphocytic) colitis after dietary trigger; surgical abdomen not fully excludable remotely

pathophysiology_summary: Lymphocytic (microscopic) colitis produces secretory, non-bloody watery diarrhoea with a normal colonoscopic appearance and intraepithelial lymphocytosis on biopsy; flares are intensely crampy but do not damage the bowel wall or cause obstruction. A rich meal triggered a severe colicky flare with explosive watery diarrhoea, which the patient fears is obstruction/perforation. The strongly reassuring discriminators are the continued passage of abundant stool and flatus, colicky (not constant) pain that eases after defecation, no vomiting, no distension, no fever, no RLQ localisation and no blood. However, telehealth cannot palpate the abdomen, so management couples symptomatic relief with a strict surgical-abdomen safety-net and a low threshold for in-person review.

differential.evidence_basis: Colicky watery-diarrhoea flare after a rich meal with known microscopic colitis.
Screen for obstipation, vomiting, distension; against it here (passing stool/wind).
No gross blood/mucus and a recent normal colonoscopy argue against a severe IBD flare.

Passing abundant stool/flatus, no vomiting/distension (DI-001), colicky pain easing after defecation with no fever/RLQ/blood (DI-002), post-rich-meal on known microscopic colitis (DI-003) — a functional colitis flare.
Continued flatus and no vomiting/distension — complete obstruction not supported, but retain a safety-net.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: antispasmodic + heat + bland diet, a strict surgical-abdomen safety-net, and a low threshold for in-person review because the abdomen cannot be palpated remotely. Neither a reflex midnight ED/CT nor unscreened reassurance is acceptable.

red_flags.description: Complete obstipation (no stool/flatus) with vomiting and distension (obstruction)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Mechanical obstruction can lead to perforation.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed obstruction progresses to strangulation/perforation.

red_flags.description: Constant severe/localised pain, fever, peritonism, or bloody stool (surgical abdomen/colitis)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Suggests appendicitis, peritonitis or a severe colitis.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed surgical abdomen leads to perforation/sepsis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Sending a dramatic colicky flare for a midnight CT/ED without screening the discriminating features.
Reassuring an acute abdomen that cannot be examined remotely, missing an early surgical cause.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Auscultation of bowel sounds and imaging
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Antispasmodic + simple measures with a strict surgical-abdomen safety-net and a low threshold for same-day in-person review; ED now if obstruction/peritonism features develop.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Either extreme: an unnecessary midnight CT, or a falsely reassured surgical abdomen.
